title: Finite sums of ambient operators
summary: Defines finite real linear combinations of rank-r ambient operators and proves that a finite sum of operators tangential at the same input weights remains tangential.

mathematicalContent.heading: Finite operator sums
mathematicalContent.body: For a finite index set S, coefficients cᵢ, and rank-r operators Pᵢ, the resulting operator is evaluated pointwise on the ambient space.

mathematicalContent.heading: Boundary stability
mathematicalContent.body: If corresponding summands agree modulo Q, their finite sums also agree modulo Q. Scalar multiplication and addition of Q-errors supply the proof.
mathematicalContent.formula: A_i\equiv B_i\pmod Q\ \forall i\in S\quad\Longrightarrow\quad\sum_{i\in S}A_i\equiv\sum_{i\in S}B_i\pmod Q.

mathematicalContent.heading: Tangentiality
mathematicalContent.body: A common multiweight is essential: when every Pᵢ is tangential at w, the entire finite linear combination is tangential at w.

correspondence.leanType: def singleOperator {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the scalar multiple of a rank-r operator.

correspondence.leanType: def operatorSum {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines a finite pointwise linear combination of rank-r operators.

correspondence.leanType: theorem singleOperator_apply {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.singleOperator c P F U = c * P F U
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates a scalar multiple at an input tuple and ambient point.

correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_apply {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.operatorSum S c P F U = ∑ i ∈ S, c i * P i F U
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the finite operator sum pointwise.

correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_empty {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.operatorSum (∅ : Finset ι) c P = CalConf.multiZero r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The operator sum over the empty support is the zero operator.

correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_singleton {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.operatorSum ({i} : Finset ι) c P = CalConf.singleOperator (c i) (P i)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A sum over a singleton is the corresponding single scalar multiple.

correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_finset_sum {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (A B : ι → Function Conf) (hAB : ∀ i ∈ S, CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (A i) (B i)) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (fun U => ∑ i ∈ S, A i U) (fun U => ∑ i ∈ S, B i U)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that finite summation preserves equality modulo the defining function Q.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \bigl(A_i\equiv B_i\pmod Q\ \forall i\in S\bigr)\Longrightarrow\sum_{i\in S}A_i\equiv\sum_{i\in S}B_i\pmod Q.

correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) {w : Fin r → ℝ} (hP : ∀ i ∈ S, CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (P i) w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.operatorSum S c P) w
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that a finite linear combination of operators tangential at one common multiweight is again tangential.

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: This module proves boundary equivalence and tangentiality of finite sums, but deliberately does not prove output homogeneity: that requires an additional linearity interface for the Euler operator X.
